description_long: This object is a decorative pennant featuring a deep reddish-brown fabric with a pointed, swallowtail bottom edge. The pennant is suspended from a horizontal rod, which appears to be made of wood or a similar material, and the rod extends slightly beyond the width of the fabric. A gold zigzag and stepped border runs along the top and bottom edges of the pennant, adding a touch of visual interest and contrast. The fabric hangs straight down, suggesting it is relatively lightweight and flexible. The overall design is simple yet elegant, suitable for ceremonial, festive, or decorative use in urban environments such as parks, plazas, or building facades. The pennant's construction and materials suggest it is intended for temporary or semi-permanent display.
description: A reddish-brown swallowtail pennant with gold zigzag borders, hanging from a horizontal rod. The design is simple and decorative, suitable for urban display.
description_view_0: Side view showing the thin profile of the pennant and the rod, with the fabric barely visible.
description_view_1: Front view displaying the full reddish-brown pennant with gold zigzag borders and the supporting rod clearly visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, showing the thin edge of the rod and fabric.
description_view_3: Rear view of the pennant, showing the same design as the front with the rod and gold borders visible.
attribute_graphic_design: The pennant features a solid reddish-brown field with gold zigzag and stepped borders at the top and bottom. There are no logos, text, or symbols present.
